_comment: Version-controlled patches re-applied to plugin CACHES at startup. A plugin cache is managed like node_modules (clobbered on every plugin update), so a useful local edit can't just be synced as a file — it must be (a) tracked here and (b) re-applied per host. The applier (apply-plugin-patches.py) is idempotent + FAIL-LOUD: it never force-applies, and emits a WARN (without failing startup) if a plugin update made a patch stale. A stale patch is a signal to re-base it — or better, upstream the change so the patch retires. See README.md.

patches.desc: discord:access — add the safer `group append` / `group rm-allow` subcommands (append-without-replace; avoids the lock-yourself-out trap of stock `group add`, which overwrites the channel allowlist). Upstream candidate for claude-plugins-official.
